op.schema: loading standard candidates for the design

schema.head: Standard ontology candidates
schema.hint: For the terms this design newly mints, here are matching terms from famous standards (suggestions only — adopt them in the dataset after ingest).

picker.placeholder: Search a standard term (e.g. crystal structure)
picker.none: No matching standard term. Try different words.

adopt.head: Ground to an external standard
adopt.hint: Map this dataset's own terms to a real term in a famous standard ontology (candidates come from a closed set; a human confirms).
adopt.noTerms: No own terms to align yet (they appear once the vocabulary is settled after ingest).

banner.title: Align your words to the world's standard words
banner.sub: Link this data's own words to commonly used standard terms. Once aligned, your data connects automatically with others, and external tools and AI understand it directly.

why.head: Why align?
why.p1: Connect to other data with the same meaning, automatically
why.p2: External tools and AI understand it directly
why.p3: Searchable by the world's common terms too

detected.head: Standards found automatically
detected.sub: Well-known vocabularies already used inside the data.

caution: Candidates are found by AI; a human confirms. Similar words can mean subtly different things, so only what you confirm gets aligned.
